judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are thorough and hit all four expectations with nearly identical technical substance (same commands, same conflict discovery, same rebase plan); Report 1 is slightly more rigorous in isolating the true feature diff via git diff feat/weighted-scorer..feat/pairwise-metric, while Report 2 adds a two-dot vs three-dot diff distinction that's a nice bonus but not required.

report_1.evidence: `main`. It's the only integration/trunk branch in the repository — ... `feat/weighted-scorer` is not a viable target: as shown in Q2, its content is already on `main`, so targeting it would aim the PR at a branch that is itself stale/superseded.

report_2.evidence: `main`. This repo has exactly one trunk ... never stack a PR on a feature branch as its base. That rule is doubly important here because the branch this one was actually cut from, `feat/weighted-scorer`, is no longer a valid target at all

report_1.evidence: This is conclusive evidence that `feat/weighted-scorer` was **squash-merged into `main`** as PR #42, under a new commit hash that git's ancestry graph has no way of linking back to the original branch.

report_2.evidence: main`'s tip `1971f08` ("Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)") touches the same two files as W1–W3 combined. ... Yet `git merge-base --is-ancestor ac9d505 main` → **NO**

report_1.evidence: the remaining 16 insertions and all 3 deletions — every change in `scoring/scorer.py`, plus 6 of the 10 insertions in `scoring/metrics.py` (the `overlap()` helper) — are `feat/weighted-scorer`'s content, present only because `feat/pairwise-metric` branched from `feat/weighted-scorer` and was never rebased after that work landed on `main` as #42.

report_2.evidence: So **no, not all of it is pairwise-metric work.** Only `X1` and `X2` (5 net lines) are. The other 3 commits/16 lines are the `feat/weighted-scorer` dependency's content, present purely because `feat/pairwise-metric` was cut from `feat/weighted-scorer` before that branch's work reached `main` through a different, non-ancestor commit.

report_1.evidence: **Rebase `feat/pairwise-metric` to drop the now-redundant `feat/weighted-scorer` commits**, replaying only the genuine pairwise work onto current `main`: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric` ... **Verify the result**: re-run `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric --stat` (should now show only 2 files / 5 insertions / 0 deletions)

report_2.evidence: **Rebase away the dead-base commits**: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`. ... **Verify the result**: `git diff main feat/pairwise-metric` should now show exactly the 5-line pairwise-only change
